description: Conduct statistical hypothesis tests through the standard six-step framework — stating hypotheses, selecting test statistic, setting significance, deciding rule, computing, and concluding

fields.name: null_hypothesis
fields.label: H0 — statement to test, typically equality or status quo

fields.name: alternative_hypothesis
fields.label: Ha — the inequality (one-sided or two-sided) the analyst wishes to demonstrate

fields.name: significance_level
fields.label: alpha — probability of Type I error (commonly 0.01, 0.05, 0.10)

fields.name: p_value
fields.label: Probability of observing a test statistic at least as extreme as observed under H0

six_step_process.step_1: State the null (H0) and alternative (Ha) hypotheses — mutually exclusive and collectively exhaustive
six_step_process.step_2: Identify the appropriate test statistic and its sampling distribution
six_step_process.step_4: State the decision rule (critical value region)
six_step_process.step_5: Collect data and calculate the test statistic
six_step_process.step_6: Make a decision: reject or fail to reject H0

error_types.type_i: Rejecting H0 when H0 is true (false positive); probability = alpha
error_types.type_ii: Failing to reject H0 when H0 is false (false negative); probability = beta
error_types.power: Probability of correctly rejecting a false H0; power = 1 - beta

significance_and_decision.equivalence: Both approaches yield the same decision at a given alpha

hypothesis_structure.two_sided: H0: theta = theta_0 vs Ha: theta != theta_0
hypothesis_structure.one_sided_upper: H0: theta <= theta_0 vs Ha: theta > theta_0
hypothesis_structure.one_sided_lower: H0: theta >= theta_0 vs Ha: theta < theta_0
hypothesis_structure.null_contains_equality: H0 always includes the equality; Ha never does

practical_guidance.alpha_selection: 0.05 is convention; tighter alpha for costlier Type I, looser for costlier Type II
practical_guidance.sample_size: Larger n increases power at fixed alpha
practical_guidance.effect_size: Statistical significance != practical significance; always report magnitude

applications.risk_model_validation: Test whether realized VaR breaches match predicted
applications.policy_changes: Test whether mean returns differ pre/post policy announcement

validation.hypotheses_mutually_exclusive: H0 and Ha must not overlap
validation.test_type_defined: Two-sided vs one-sided must be declared BEFORE seeing data

reject_null_critical_value.description: Test statistic exceeds critical value — reject H0

fail_to_reject.description: Insufficient evidence — fail to reject H0

errors.message: Significance level alpha must be strictly between 0 and 1
Both null and alternative hypotheses must be specified

anti_patterns.rule: Do not select the test type (one-sided vs two-sided) after observing the data
anti_patterns.why: Post-hoc sidedness selection doubles the effective Type I error rate; specify the alternative hypothesis before data collection

anti_patterns.rule: Do not confuse 'failing to reject H0' with 'accepting H0'
anti_patterns.why: Failure to reject means insufficient evidence, not confirmation; the null may still be false with the power of the test too low to detect it

anti_patterns.rule: Do not p-hack by running multiple tests and reporting only significant results
anti_patterns.why: Multiple testing inflates family-wise Type I error; apply Bonferroni or FDR corrections, or pre-register the hypothesis

anti_patterns.rule: Do not equate statistical significance with economic significance
anti_patterns.why: Large samples produce significant p-values for trivially small effects; always report effect size and confidence interval alongside p-value
